title: Drip Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Drip** is an email-marketing + automation platform exposing a v2 REST API under `https://api.getdrip.com/v2/{account_id}`. This connector ŌĆö `DripConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "drip"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) ŌĆö wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from a Drip account:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Subscribers | `/subscribers` | List + get + create-or-update + delete |
| Tags | `/tags` + `/subscribers/{id}/tags/{tag}` | List, apply, remove |
| Events | `/events` | Record custom events tied to a subscriber email |
| Orders | `/orders` | List + create (triggers Ecommerce automations) |
| Campaigns | `/campaigns` | List + get, subscribe an email to one |
| Workflows | `/workflows` | List, trigger for a subscriber email |
| Custom Fields | `/custom_field_identifiers` | List custom field keys |
| Broadcasts | `/broadcasts` | List one-off email broadcasts |
| Forms | `/forms` | List email-capture forms |

The connector normalises subscribers + campaigns + orders into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per spec operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx + transport errors with exponential backoff (3 attempts at the HTTP layer), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC ŌĆö all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::DripHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate Drip credentials

1. Sign in to https://www.getdrip.com.
2. Open **Settings ŌåÆ General Info** and copy the numeric **Account ID** (also visible as the URL prefix `app.getdrip.com/{id}/ŌĆ”`).
3. From the avatar menu open **User Settings ŌåÆ My User Settings**.
4. In the **API Token** section copy the token (or generate a new one). Treat it as a password ŌĆö anyone with the token can read and mutate every subscriber, campaign, and workflow in the account.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP ŌåÆ Manage Connectors ŌåÆ Drip**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `api_key` (secret) | API key from step 4 |
| `account_id` (text) | Numeric account id from step 2 |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://api.getdrip.com/v2` (rarely needed) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 3600 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates the fields, persists config via `save_config`, then round-trips `GET /campaigns` so an invalid key fails fast.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which lists campaigns as a lightweight probe. On 2xx ŌåÆ `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401/403 ŌåÆ `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED` (or, at install time, `OFFLINE + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`). On 429 ŌåÆ retried with exponential backoff; if still failing ŌåÆ `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Drip uses **HTTP Basic authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry.

### Credential model

- `api_key` is sent as the **username** of an HTTP Basic pair, with an **empty password** ŌĆö encoded as `Basic base64(api_key + ":")`.
- `account_id` is appended to the base URL: `https://api.getdrip.com/v2/{account_id}`.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `api_key` + `account_id`. Persists config. Round-trips `GET /campaigns` ŌĆö install fails fast if the key is wrong. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a synthetic `TokenInfo(access_token=api_key, token_type="Basic")` for ABI uniformity. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /campaigns` ŌĆö minimal probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called ŌĆö no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
Authorization: Basic base64(api_key:)
Accept:        application/json
Content-Type:  application/vnd.api+json
User-Agent:    Shielva-Drip-Connector/1.0
```

### Back-compat

Earlier revisions of this connector used the config key `api_token`. `__init__` accepts either `api_key` (canonical) or `api_token` (legacy) so previously-installed tenants keep working without a re-install.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` ŌĆö never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `api_key` | secret | yes | `self.api_key` | Basic-auth username (empty password). Falls back to `api_token` for legacy installs. |
| `account_id` | text | yes | `self.account_id` | Appended to `base_url` to form the effective host. |
| `base_url` | text | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `https://api.getdrip.com/v2`) | Override for staging / proxy. |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 3600) | Client-side soft cap. |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["api_key", "account_id"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_DRIP_BASE_HOST = "https://api.getdrip.com/v2"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `DripConnector`. SOC: `connector.py` orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::DripHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `api_key` + `account_id`; persist config; round-trip `GET /campaigns`.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth ŌĆö returns a synthetic `TokenInfo` so platform plumbing has a uniform shape.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Lists campaigns via `GET /campaigns`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterates Drip subscribers (page-paged) + campaigns ŌåÆ `NormalizedDocument` ŌåÆ `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Subscribers
children.content:
### `async list_subscribers(*, status="active", page=1, per_page=50, subscribed_after=None, subscribed_before=None, tags=None) -> Dict`
`GET /subscribers` ŌĆö page-based pagination via `meta.total_pages`.

### `async get_subscriber(id_or_email) -> Dict`
`GET /subscribers/{id_or_email}` ŌĆö URL-encodes `@`/`+`/etc so emails work in the path slot.

### `async create_or_update_subscriber(email, *, custom_fields=None, tags=None, time_zone=None, ip_address=None, first_name=None, last_name=None) -> Dict`
`POST /subscribers` ŌĆö body wrapped in `{subscribers:[ŌĆ”]}` envelope. Drip dedupes on email.

### `async delete_subscriber(id_or_email) -> Dict`
`DELETE /subscribers/{id_or_email}`.

children.title: Tags + Events
children.content:
### `async list_tags() -> Dict`
`GET /tags` ŌĆö all account tags.

### `async apply_tag(email, tag) -> Dict`
`POST /tags` ŌĆö body `{tags:[{email,tag}]}`.

### `async remove_tag(email, tag) -> Dict`
`DELETE /subscribers/{email}/tags/{tag}` ŌĆö both segments URL-encoded.

### `async record_event(email, action, *, properties=None, occurred_at=None) -> Dict`
`POST /events` ŌĆö body wrapped in `{events:[{email, action, properties, occurred_at}]}` envelope.

children.title: Orders + Campaigns
children.content:
### `async list_orders(*, page=1, per_page=50, occurred_after=None) -> Dict`
`GET /orders`.

### `async create_order(email, *, provider=None, provider_order_id=None, amount=None, currency=None, occurred_at=None, items=None) -> Dict`
`POST /orders` ŌĆö body wrapped in `{orders:[ŌĆ”]}`. Triggers Ecommerce automations server-side.

### `async list_campaigns(*, status="active", page=1, per_page=50) -> Dict`
`GET /campaigns`.

### `async get_campaign(campaign_id) -> Dict`
`GET /campaigns/{id}`.

### `async subscribe_to_campaign(campaign_id, email, *, double_optin=False) -> Dict`
`POST /campaigns/{id}/subscribers` ŌĆö body `{subscribers:[{email, double_optin}]}`.

children.title: Workflows ┬Ę Custom Fields ┬Ę Broadcasts ┬Ę Forms
children.content:
### `async list_workflows(*, page=1, per_page=50) -> Dict`
`GET /workflows`.

### `async trigger_workflow(workflow_id, email) -> Dict`
`POST /workflows/{id}/subscribers` ŌĆö body `{subscribers:[{email}]}`.

### `async list_custom_fields() -> Dict`
`GET /custom_field_identifiers`.

### `async list_broadcasts(*, status="draft", page=1) -> Dict`
`GET /broadcasts`.

### `async list_forms() -> Dict`
`GET /forms`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
DripError                            # base; carries status_code + response_body
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ DripAuthError                    # 401 / 403
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ DripBadRequestError              # 400
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ DripNotFoundError                # 404
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ DripConflictError                # 409
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ DripUnprocessableError           # 422
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ DripRateLimitError               # 429 ŌĆö retry_after_s honoured
ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ DripServerError                  # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
DripNetworkError = DripServerError   # legacy import name in older callers
DripNotFound     = DripNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 / 422 | Raise immediately (no retry) |
| 429 | Exponential backoff `_BACKOFF_BASE * 2 ** attempt` (0.5s, 1s, 2s), retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `DripNetworkError` |

Caller-side `helpers.utils.with_retry` adds a belt-and-braces retry layer for the same status set so an isolated 429 that the HTTP layer surfaces never blows up a sync.

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 / 403 ŌåÆ ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)
5xx / net  ŌåÆ ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,  CONNECTED)
other      ŌåÆ ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

At `install()` time a 401/403 round-trip surfaces as `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` ŌĆö installer fails fast rather than persist a bad token.

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `api_key` or `account_id` blank | Re-enter both in ACP. Drip shows the key in the User Settings panel ŌĆö copy verbatim. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at install | 401/403 from Drip ŌĆö API key wrong, revoked, or `account_id` mismatched | Confirm both values. Rotate the key in **User Settings ŌåÆ API Token** if compromised. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | API key was revoked after install | Re-install with a fresh key. |
| `drip.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (Drip's 3,600/min cap) | The client retries with exponential backoff. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer requests. |
| 404 on `/subscribers/{email}` | Email not in Drip yet | Call `create_or_update_subscriber` first. |
| 422 on `record_event` | Action name contains illegal characters, or properties contain non-JSON values | Sanitize the action string; ensure all values in `properties` are JSON-serialisable scalars. |
| 422 on `create_order` | `provider_order_id` already exists for this provider | Use a unique id per order; Drip rejects re-submits of the same `(provider, provider_order_id)` pair. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'DripNetworkError'` | Older `exceptions.py` without back-compat aliases | Already fixed ŌĆö `DripNetworkError = DripServerError` alias is in `exceptions.py`. |
